revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishizukayama Kofun]][[ja:石塚山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Ishiyamazuka Kofun | native_name =石塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ishiyamazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.77538889|N|130.98|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en|Kanda, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206876|lt=Kanda, Fukuoka|1=Kanda, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|46|31.4|N|130|58|48.0|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ishiyamazuka Kofun'''|石塚山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Tamiku neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en|Kanda, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206876|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District|1=Miyako District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2687|title=石塚山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Along with the [[Goshoyama Kofun]] (also located in Kanda) it is one of the oldest and largest Kofuns in the {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|qid=Q1047134|lt=Buzen region|1=Buzen Province}}, and is estimated to have been constructed in the early Kofun period..
==Overview== The Ishiyamazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a low hill overlooking the Gulf of Suo on the [[Seto Inland Sea]] in eastern Fukuoka Prefecture. It was very close to the coastline at that time, with the front facing toward the sea. The surrounding area has been slightly carved out, and the total length is estimated to be approximately 130 meters, and the height of the circular three-tier posterior portion part is approximately 17 meters with a diameter of 70 meters. This surface was leveled when a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. The anterior rectangular portion extends long and narrow from the constriction and widens slightly at the tip, which is a style consistent with the beginning of the 4th century. The tumulus has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but existence of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has not been confirmed.
The pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in 1796, but was subsequently filled-in. Surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include seven {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s in six different designs, a large iron sword with ring pommel, and a bronze arrowhead. These artifacts are kept at the Ubara Shrine. However, according to the records of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|qid=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, who were ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Kokura Domain|en|Kokura Domain|qid=Q1140943|lt=Kokura Domain|1=Kokura Domain}} at the time, a total of eleven bronze mirrors had been excavated, and the records of Ubara Shrine state that a total of 14 bronze mirrors had been donated to the shrine. The whereabouts of these additional mirrors in unknown. The existing mirrors are identical to those excavated from the [[Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} and the [[Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The surviving grave goods from this tumulus were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1953.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/187272|title=豊前国京都郡石塚山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Re-excavation was carried out in 1987, and it was found that the burial chamber was severely damaged. During the excavations at this time, items such as a thin wire type beast band mirror piece, an amber ''[[magatama]],'' a jasper bead, and a small leather-bound helmet piece were unearthed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 15-minutes on foot from {{ill|Kanda Station (Fukuoka) |en|Kanda Station (Fukuoka) |qid=Q5360826|lt=Kanda Station|1=Kanda Station (Fukuoka)}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanda.lg.jp/_3861/_39dd/_3929.html Kanda town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=35 Cultural properties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13172 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}
